content:
- Read the prior Choose-Task progress log and inspected .ralph/current_tasks.md plus .ralph/current_tasks_done.md.
- Confirmed there are no active bug tasks listed, no visible <blocked_by> tags on candidate task files, and that the top unblocked failing task is the in-progress high-priority docs reference-pages task.
- Next step is to write .ralph/tasks/story-build-docs-diataxis-from-zero/02-task-run-reference-pages-through-draft-check-edit-revise.md into .ralph/current_task.txt and stop immediately.

- Read .ralph/current_task.txt and found the active task: .ralph/tasks/story-build-docs-diataxis-from-zero/02-task-run-reference-pages-through-draft-check-edit-revise.md
- progress_read returned no visible prior notes, so I am treating this as a fresh context window with only repo state on disk.
- Next I am inspecting the task file to determine whether I must write the first plan, revise a TO BE VERIFIED plan, or execute a NOW EXECUTE plan.

- The task file is already in NOW EXECUTE state, so I must execute the reviewed plan rather than re-plan.
- I am loading the required ask-k2-docs and update-docs skills plus the mandatory Diataxis rereads and task 01 before selecting this run's docs slice.
- I am also checking git status, the docs tree, SUMMARY, and src/lib.rs to rebuild the live documentation gap map from repository state instead of memory.

- I selected the CLI reference slice for this run because it is a stable machinery boundary and is small enough to stay inside the docs-update cap.
- Existing pgtuskmasterctl docs already look detailed, so I am checking them skeptically for route drift and other stale claims against src/cli, src/api, and the API tests before asking K2 for any revisions.
- The likely edit set is two to three pages maximum, plus SUMMARY only if navigation needs correction.

- I sent two source-rich ask-k2-docs prompts: one for docs/src/reference/pgtuskmasterctl.md and one for docs/src/reference/pgtuskmaster.md.
- The prompts explicitly constrained K2 to reference form, ASCII punctuation, no invented facts, and the exact verified CLI/runtime surfaces from the code and tests.
- While waiting, I am keeping the edit scope to these pages unless the generated prose exposes a factual navigation correction.

- My first combined patch failed because the pgtuskmaster page content on disk did not match the stale context I had in memory.
- I am re-reading both target pages and will apply a narrower patch that matches the live files instead of forcing a destructive overwrite.
- This keeps the docs slice isolated and avoids trampling unrelated edits.

- Patched docs/src/reference/pgtuskmaster.md and docs/src/reference/pgtuskmasterctl.md with K2-authored reference rewrites plus tiny factual cleanup.
- I preserved the CLI/runtime facts from the code, but tightened the pages toward cleaner machinery-led reference structure.
- I am now running the docs-only validation commands required by this task: make docs-lint followed by make docs-build.

- Completed one capped execution slice of the reference-chapter task by revising two pages: docs/src/reference/pgtuskmaster.md and docs/src/reference/pgtuskmasterctl.md.
- Validation passed for this task's allowed commands: make docs-lint and make docs-build.
- I did not run make check/make test/make test-long/make lint because this task's contract explicitly forbids those during execution; the task remains open with <passes>false</passes>.

- Entered NOW EXECUTE for the active reference-docs task.
- Need to follow the reviewed plan exactly: reread Diataxis/task 01, inspect worktree/docs tree, choose next <=10-page slice, use ask-k2-docs for prose, use update-docs for revisions, validate with docs-only commands.
- Starting with mandatory rereads and repo baseline collection.

- Chose the next docs slice: revise docs/src/reference/http-api.md and docs/src/reference/debug-api.md.
- Gathered exact route/auth/TLS/payload/debug facts from src/api/*, src/debug_api/*, runtime wiring, and the API/debug tests.
- Fired two ask-k2-docs generations in parallel with source-rich prompts; next step is skeptical review and patching.

- The debug-api K2 call returned usable markdown.
- The first http-api K2 session appears wedged with no output after extended waits, so I am rerunning only that generation instead of blocking the slice.
- After the replacement output returns I will do the skeptical review and patch both pages together.

- Patched docs/src/reference/http-api.md and docs/src/reference/debug-api.md from the K2 drafts, keeping reference-only structure and correcting small fact/label issues during the skeptical pass.
- Next I am validating this capped docs slice with make docs-lint and make docs-build, which are the only commands allowed by this task.
- If either command fails, I will fix only the in-scope docs issues and stop after this run.

- Loaded previous progress log and confirmed the active task path from .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Reading the task file now to determine whether the task is still in planning review, or already in NOW EXECUTE.
- Need to obey the task-state marker exactly and keep appending progress as I go.

- The active task file is already in NOW EXECUTE, so I must follow the reviewed execution plan and keep the work capped.
- Reloading the mandatory Diataxis/reference guidance and task 01 now before selecting the next docs slice.
- After that I will inspect git status, SUMMARY, docs/src tree, and src/lib.rs to choose the next real machinery-backed reference pages.

- Reloaded the Diataxis sources and task 01; the live task remains in NOW EXECUTE.
- Inspecting git status, the docs/src tree, SUMMARY, and src/lib.rs now to choose the next capped reference slice from current repo state.
- I need to pick an execution slice that matches a real machinery boundary and then use ask-k2-docs/update-docs for the actual prose work.

- Chose the next execution slice: docs/src/reference/node-runtime.md and docs/src/reference/process-worker.md.
- The choice is machinery-led and inside the per-run cap; both pages map directly to src/runtime/node.rs and src/process/*.
- Gathering exact symbols, behaviors, and tests from those modules now so the K2 prompts stay source-backed and reference-only.
